 {"id":"ft-0v48y","title":"[HIGH] FrankenSqlite recorder backend is a stub — config-selecting it fails at bootstrap (recorder_storage.rs:577)","description":"Agent A mock-code-finder: FrankenSqlite recorder backend returns BackendUnavailable; only AppendLog is wired. If config.storage.recorder_backend=frankensqlite, bootstrap fails cleanly but the feature surface is entirely missing. Cross-ref: config.rs:879 rejects same value at config load. Either (a) mark frankensqlite backend as 'planned' in docs and in the config parser (hide the variant), or (b) implement the backend (see wa-2l27x.8 FrankenSQLite migration plan).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:50:10.136429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:53:49.916655Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:53:49.916340Z","close_reason":"Shipped in fe0e2ca3: default builds reject frankensqlite recorder selection behind non-default feature gate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","mock","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-0vt3k","title":"[SEV] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology rollback check only compares pane id set, not split geometry","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology Phase 5 (rewritten in 30172171 to fix ft-olq2e) now derives post_rollback_pane_ids from server.registry().snapshot() and asserts the sorted id list matches baseline_pane_ids. This is stronger than the previous vacuous baseline-JSON roundtrip, but it still only validates the *set* of surviving pane ids, not the restored split tree geometry: split ratios, split directions (horizontal vs vertical), parent/child edges, pane widths/heights, or the z-order of panes within the topology. A broken rollback that restores the correct 2 pane ids but swaps their split direction, scales their sizes, or re-parents them under a different split node would still pass this assertion. Metamorphic invariant: TopologySnapshot::from_registry(post_rollback) == TopologySnapshot::from_registry(pre_expansion) — not currently asserted. Fix by capturing a full TopologySnapshot before Phase 3 expansion, then after rollback compare structural fields (split tree, ratios, per-pane dimensions) in addition to pane id set. The existing 'restored_topo = from_json(&initial_topo_json)' on line 422 still only proves serde roundtrip; it does not compare to the live post-rollback state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:42.691288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912477Z","close_reason":"Resolved in 4784c64f + d29498d6. d29498d6 added TopologyFingerprint covering HSplit/VSplit direction, per-split child ratios (encode_ratio to u16), per-leaf pane_id/rows/cols, tab_id, active_pane_id, window_id, and active_tab_index; Phase 5 now asserts post_rollback_fingerprint == initial_fingerprint AND != expanded_fingerprint, so a rollback that restores the correct pane id set but swaps split direction, rescales panes, or re-parents nodes fails. 4784c64f tightened the check further with a direct TopologySnapshot equality (post_rollback_topo == &initial_topo) as the primary assertion and baseline-JSON == initial_topo as the secondary. Ran the test at HEAD: binary compiles clean; runtime verification on local host blocked by macOS dyld bottleneck under concurrent-agent compile load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-0wnq3","title":"[audit] patterns: detect_with_context allocates detections before overlap and dedupe gates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:40.555690Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:53:07.550298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:33.823088Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current checkout: detect_with_context gates on agent type, overlap, and dedupe before materialization, with focused ft-0wnq3 regression tests already present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3611,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perf finding from 2026-04-23 hot-path audit: detect_with_context() materializes all candidate Detection objects before overlap, agent-type, and dedupe gating, so the steady-state path pays allocation and cloning cost for matches that are immediately discarded. Evidence: detect() allocates Vec<Detection> and, for each regex hit, clones rule_id/event_type, builds extracted JSON, and allocates matched_text at patterns.rs:2232-2323; detect_with_context() then calls detect(&input_text) and only afterwards drops overlap-window matches, wrong-agent rules, and already-seen detections at patterns.rs:2375-2409. Worst case: a noisy pane with large tail overlap or repeated limit banners causes many regex captures to allocate Strings/Maps only to be filtered out, scaling cost with candidate matches rather than emitted detections. Proposed measurement strategy: add a criterion bench around detect_with_context() with (1) high-overlap segmented inputs, (2) repeated duplicate banners under a warm DetectionContext, and (3) mixed-agent rule packs; record wall time plus allocation counts/bytes via heaptrack or dhat, and compare candidate detection count vs emitted count to quantify wasted work before any fix.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:49Z"},{"id":3612,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current HEAD already avoids the reported hot-path waste. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:2442-2468, agent-type / overlap / dedupe checks all  before any allocation-heavy materialization; , , and  construction happen only afterward at :2470-2487 and :2500-2511. Focused regression tests are already present in the same file:  and  around :4769-4826.  attributes the reordered gating/materialization path to commit 5dcea11e (2026-04-23). No code change needed in this checkout; switching to ft-8ku8p.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:11Z"},{"id":3613,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current HEAD already avoids the reported hot-path waste. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs lines 2442-2468, agent-type, overlap, and dedupe checks all continue before any allocation-heavy materialization. extract_captures, matched_text, and Detection construction happen only afterward at lines 2470-2487 and 2500-2511. Focused regression tests are already present in the same file: dedup_drop_path_allocations_do_not_scale_with_matched_text_ft_0wnq3 and overlap_drop_path_skips_detection_materialization_ft_0wnq3 around lines 4769-4826. git blame attributes the reordered gating/materialization path to commit 5dcea11e from 2026-04-23. No code change needed in this checkout; switching to ft-8ku8p.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:26Z"},{"id":3615,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Correction: the immediately preceding comment with blank placeholders was a failed shell-quoted attempt. The accurate evidence is in the next comment: current HEAD gates before materialization, and the focused ft-0wnq3 regression tests are already present.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:53:07Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-0zoq3","title":"[profiling] TOON encoder: README sells token-efficiency but toon_rust::encode hot path has zero benches","description":"## Hypothesis

`toon_rust::encode(value, None)` (called from
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_middleware.rs:90` and the MCP
helpers) is on the hot path of every `format=toon` robot/MCP
response. README sells it as the token-efficient AI-to-AI output.

There is **no bench** for it. `grep -lE "toon" benches/*.rs`
returns zero hits. The serialization/encoding cost per kilobyte
of pane state, plus the relative cost vs `serde_json::to_string`
and the cost of `try_decode` on the consumer side, is invisible.

## Why a profile + bench would deliver real perf gain

A swarm operating 200+ panes calls `wa.state` repeatedly, with
TOON output. If TOON encoding is even 2-3× slower than JSON, the
"token-efficient" framing is a poor tradeoff at swarm scale (you
trade encoder CPU on the ft side for decoder savings on the
agent side). Today no one has measured this:

- Encoder CPU per KB of pane-state Value
- Decoder CPU on the round-trip
- Output size (tokens vs JSON) at the realistic envelope shapes
  (`wa.state`, `wa.search`, `wa.events`)
- Allocation profile (does TOON allocate per cell? per row?)

## Suggested instrumentation + bench

1. **Bench**: `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/toon_encoding.rs`,
   criterion harness with three workloads:
   - `wa.state` payload at 1, 10, 50, 200 panes (matches the
     scaling-law disciplines — p95 ratio across pane counts).
   - `wa.search` payload at 10, 100, 1000 hits.
   - `wa.events` payload at 10, 100, 1000 events.
   For each: `serde_json::to_string` baseline + `toon_rust::encode`
   side-by-side. Track p50/p95/p99 + bytes/op.

2. **Instrumentation**: `tracing::instrument(skip_all)` on the
   MCP middleware encode call site, with a `format` field tag.
   Histogram: `metrics::histogram!("mcp.encode_ms", ..., format)`.

3. **Profile workload**: 200-pane `wa.state` + `wa.search` rotation
   for 30 seconds under `samply record`, look for the encoder /
   serializer split.

## Hypothesis ledger entries

```
toon-encoder-cpu-dominates : pending — measure encode_ms p95
                              before claiming dominant
serde-json-faster-encode  : pending — JSON p95 < TOON p95 ?
allocation-per-cell       : pending — dhat shows alloc count per
                              encode call?
output-size-savings-real  : pending — bytes/op TOON vs JSON at
                              200-pane wa.state
```

## Out of scope

- Optimizing TOON itself (separate bead via
  /extreme-software-optimization once the bench exists).
- The fastmcp framework overhead (covered by
  `framework_throughput.rs`).

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/toon_encoding.rs` exists and is
  registered in Cargo.toml.
- Three workloads (`wa.state`, `wa.search`, `wa.events`) at four
  scales each, both JSON + TOON.
- A `tests/artifacts/perf/toon-encoding-<run-id>/` artifact with
  fingerprint.json + hotspot table after a samply run.
- Hypothesis ledger committed under `docs/perf-ledger/` with the
  four entries above marked `supports` / `rejects`.

## References

- Skill: profiling-software-performance — Hand-off contract
  (ranked hotspot table required before any optimization)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_middleware.rs:90` (call site)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:1090-1092` (round-trip use)
- README §TL;DR (TOON's "token-efficient AI-to-AI" claim)

Filed by cc_1 profiling audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:44:37.381312Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T11:01:06.796675Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T11:01:06.796513Z","close_reason":"added TOON encoder criterion baseline, ledger, and perf artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-12e8l","title":"[SEV] mux-server Alert-after-PaneRemoved re-inserts HashMap entry, permanent leak","description":"crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs:256 processes MuxNotification::Alert by calling handler.per_pane(pane_id), which routes to SessionHandler::per_pane at sessionhandler.rs:282. That function uses HashMap::entry(..).or_insert_with(..) — a get-or-insert. When a MuxNotification::Alert arrives for a pane whose entry was already cleared by Item::Notif(MuxNotification::PaneRemoved) at dispatch.rs:247, the or_insert_with re-creates a fresh PerPane entry. The pane is already gone from mux, so no subsequent PaneRemoved will fire for that pane_id — the entry is a permanent leak.

Concrete interleaving:
  1. Mux fires PaneRemoved(1) followed by (or interleaved with) a delayed Alert{pane_id:1, PaletteChanged} that was enqueued by a config-reload path or a termwiz late-palette flush.
  2. Dispatch loop processes PaneRemoved -> handler.remove_per_pane(1) clears the map entry (line 247).
  3. Dispatch loop processes Alert -> handler.per_pane(1) -> or_insert_with -> re-creates an entry (line 256).
  4. Line 262 schedule_pane_push(1) likewise calls per_pane() internally, reinforcing the stale entry.
  5. No further PaneRemoved for pane 1 is ever emitted (pane already killed). Entry leaks for the lifetime of the ClientSession.

Severity: unbounded growth proportional to (alerts-after-removal × lifetime). Long-lived mux-server clients (editor sessions, persistent SSH mux) with high pane churn accumulate stale entries. Each entry holds a PerPane with notifications Vec, RenderableDimensions, and SurfaceCopy — not large individually but multiplied by churn + uptime, it matters.

Fix: expose a non-inserting accessor SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present(pane_id) -> Option<Arc<Mutex<PerPane>>> and use it for the Alert arm. If the pane isn't tracked, drop the Alert (the client can't render a dead pane anyway). Keep the or_insert_with pathway for client-initiated PDU arms (SendKeyDown, SendPaste, etc.) where insertion on first reference is correct. Also skip the schedule_pane_push for a pane that was just removed (line 262) — same reasoning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:46:06.681760Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407372Z","close_reason":"Completed: added SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present non-inserting accessor and routed dispatch.rs Alert arm through it. Alert-after-PaneRemoved now drops the stale alert silently instead of re-creating a permanent map entry. Code changes landed in commit 3b45408d (another agent's codec-fuzz commit bundled my dispatch.rs + sessionhandler.rs edits into their push — my 37 lines are present in HEAD). cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --offline passed clean in 13.28s during my local verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1bytz","title":"[mock-finder] Fleet scrollback pressure eviction is advisory only","description":"Production defect found by mock-code-finder in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:2025 wires SnapshotPaneScrollbackAccess into the runtime fleet scrollback coordinator.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_scrollback_coordinator.rs:529-556 defines the production SnapshotPaneScrollbackAccess adapter and its evict_warm_pages/evict_all_warm methods return 0/no-op.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_scrollback_coordinator.rs:434 calls panes.evict_all_warm(info.pane_id) during emergency pressure handling.

Impact:
The fleet scrollback coordinator can report and decide on emergency warm-tier eviction, but the production runtime adapter cannot reclaim any warm pages. Under memory pressure the action is a placeholder/no-op while telemetry can imply eviction was attempted.

Expected fix:
Add a real runtime-side warm-eviction mutation hook to the vendored/session scrollback backend, or make the coordinator surface an explicit unsupported/advisory status instead of pretending eviction happened. Add a regression that emergency pressure changes warm bytes or emits a truthful unsupported action record.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:08:52.803409Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:40:42.654611Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:40:42.654449Z","close_reason":"Enforced snapshot-backed fleet scrollback eviction and verified fleet coordinator tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.28","title":"[gui][test] GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite","description":"# GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite

## What
Automated tests for the wgpu rendering pipeline: correctness, visual regression detection, and performance validation.

## Why
GPU rendering bugs are invisible to unit tests — they produce wrong pixels, not wrong values. We need screenshot-based comparison to catch rendering regressions in text layout, cursor position, selection highlighting, split pane borders, and floating pane overlays.

## Tests

### Unit Tests (no GPU required)
- `test_cell_vertex_generation` — cell (row, col) produces correct vertex positions
- `test_atlas_rect_lookup` — glyph lookup returns correct UV coordinates
- `test_atlas_eviction_lru` — LRU evicts least-recently-used glyphs
- `test_dirty_rect_merge` — adjacent dirty cells merge into single rect
- `test_dirty_rect_clear_on_present` — dirty rects cleared after frame

### Visual Regression Tests (headless GPU or software renderer)
- `visual_basic_text` — "Hello, World!" renders correctly
- `visual_unicode_cjk` — CJK characters render at double width
- `visual_emoji` — emoji renders correctly (with fallback glyph)
- `visual_box_drawing` — box-drawing characters connect seamlessly
- `visual_cursor_shapes` — block, bar, underline cursor shapes
- `visual_selection_highlight` — selected text has correct background color
- `visual_split_pane_borders` — split pane borders render as clean lines
- `visual_floating_pane` — floating pane renders with shadow/border above tiled panes
- `visual_50_panes` — 50 split panes all render legible text

### Performance Tests
- `perf_frame_time_single_pane` — full-screen pane, measure frame time (target: < 4ms)
- `perf_frame_time_50_panes` — 50 panes, measure frame time (target: < 16ms)
- `perf_atlas_warmup` — time to cache 200 unique glyphs (target: < 100ms)
- `perf_scroll_fps` — continuous scroll, measure sustained fps (target: 60fps)

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_gpu_rendering.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui in headless mode (--headless)
2. Open shell, run: echo "RENDER_TEST_$(date +%s)"
3. Capture screenshot to /tmp/ft_visual_test.png
4. Compare against golden screenshot (perceptual hash, tolerance for font differences)
5. Open 10 split panes, capture screenshot
6. Verify all 10 panes contain visible text (OCR or pixel sampling)
7. Measure frame time over 100 frames, report p50/p95/p99
8. Report: pass/fail with visual diff images on failure
```

## Golden Screenshot Management
- Golden screenshots stored in `tests/golden/gpu/`
- Updated via `BLESS=1 cargo test` (like insta crate)
- Platform-specific goldens: `tests/golden/gpu/macos/`, `tests/golden/gpu/linux/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visual regression suite runs in CI headlessly
- Frame time p99 < 16ms for single pane
- Font atlas handles full Unicode BMP without crash
- Visual diff images attached to failing CI runs for diagnosis","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"greyfalcon","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.252309Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:37:45.995027Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:37:45.994795Z","close_reason":"Bounded split-divider render geometry test landed in bf9db5d5; remaining visual harness tracked by ft-ombfl","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","gui","rendering","testing"],"comments":[{"id":2635,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote taking a bounded support slice under the blocked GPU regression-suite bead: expand pure renderer helper/unit coverage in existing test modules (`termwindow/webgpu.rs` and `termwindow/render/mod.rs`) without pretending the full headless screenshot suite is ready before `ft-1memj.10` stabilizes. Goal: tighten edge-case coverage around surface-format/extent selection and render color/cursor helper invariants so the eventual visual-regression harness has a firmer correctness floor.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:45:45Z"},{"id":2637,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote progress: added focused renderer helper edge-case tests in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs. New coverage pins alpha-mode empty-capability fallback, per-axis surface extent clamping, explicit palette-vs-style foreground precedence, bright ANSI palette handling under bold intensity, and reverse-video cursor exact-threshold semantics. Validation: `cargo fmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed. `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-gui termwindow::webgpu::tests` failed on worker `vmi1293453` with `RCH-E307` because `x11.pc` is missing from the worker image; companion `termwindow::render::tests` run fell open local after `RCH-E324` and then stalled in an uninterruptible wait, so I terminated that process rather than leave a useless local compile hanging. Bead remains in_progress because the full GPU regression suite still depends on ft-1memj.10 and proper worker images.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:52:53Z"},{"id":3521,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook taking a bounded ft-1memj.28 slice while Agent Mail is timing out under DB contention. Scope: audit the existing GPU renderer helper test surface in crates/frankenterm-gui and land a non-overlapping correctness/regression slice that improves renderer invariants without pretending the full headless visual suite is ready.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:27:47Z"},{"id":3526,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large and independent-axis clamping. Remote targeted verification reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing x11-xcb.pc for crate x11. Local rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check are clean.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:37:10Z"},{"id":3527,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live-resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large/independent-axis clamping.
